dishes: Classic Pork Minced and Egg Cheong Fun
Diced Chicken with Pickled Vegetables with Rice
reviews: There seems to have been some mention on social media recently on a stall serving up Cantonese-style Stone Mill Cheong Fun at Redhill Food Centre... The menu revolves around their Cantonese Stone Mill Cheong Fun, offering patrons with different variants of the dish that comes with various filling options...
stalls.stall_name: 正其广式石磨肠粉 Cantonese Stone Mill Cheong Fun (Redhill Food Centre)

google.google_name: 广式石磨肠粉 Cantonese Stone Mill Cheong Fun

reviews.author: Casey Shanks
reviews.text: Chee cheong fun feels light, thinner than typical Hong Kong ones, yet hearty at the same time. Savoury fillings were nicely paired with eggs and greens. It’s a meal in itself. There is a sprinkle of pickled-something that gives the dish a kick in a very nice direction. The chili condiment is also very fitting if you like some fire in your bites.

reviews.author: S L
reviews.text:
China-style cheong fun! A bit different from those I had in Shenzhen though.

Paynow and CDC available. Friendly lady boss.

reviews.author: Nick Koh
reviews.text: Egg and minced meat

reviews.author: Ai M
reviews.text:
Cantonese-style Cheong Fun at Redhill FC!

I ordered No. 3 from the Signature Menu: Classic Minced Pork and Egg Cheong Fun.
This cheong fun is vibrantly colored, with toppings, lettuce, and a sprinkling of sesame flakes and carrots.

The serving size of the cheong fun is wider than the typical rectangular size, making it feel more substantial. It's just the right amount to fill you up without overeating.

The cheong fun is truly delicious.
The flavor is mild and not heavy, and the beautiful egg yolk complements the cheong fun, further increasing the feeling of fullness.

The cheong fun is made with homemade rice flour!

If I may be so picky, I'd say the cheong fun sauce could be improved for a more satisfying overall taste.
The light soy sauce base is good and blends well with the cheong fun, but it leaves a slight, unpleasant aftertaste. The cheong fun itself is incredibly delicious, but the aftertaste of the sauce is disappointing. I'm probably the only one who finds this annoying, but if it were to disappear, it would definitely be the most delicious cheong fun ever!
While I'm thinking this, I might just have a good sense of taste, so I'll definitely try this cheong fun again next time I have breakfast at Redhill. It's still delicious.


Use the hawker center's toilets.

reviews.author: Chao Liang
reviews.text: The owner told me that the broth for the rice noodle rolls doesn't contain MSG, but it seems they're making improvements based on customer requests 😆😆 Personally, I still think it's delicious 😋

reviews: Steamed Chicken (half) Rice Chicken Liver x 3 Chicken Gizzards x 3... Special Combi Steam Chicken + Roasted Chicken + Chicken Cutlet + Rice Such huge serving!
“Three Combination” set, which allows you to choose three types of meat.
stalls.stall_name: SP3 Chicken Rice (Redhill Lane Block 85 Food Centre)

google.google_name: 荣记传统海南鸡饭Rongji Chicken Rice @ Redhill 85

reviews.author: Ivan Tan
reviews.text: Happened to be here as was looking for something to eat. Chicken rice and that was what i ordered. It’s no 1 which is the steamed chicken and ordered the large since they have it at $6.50 and added an extra egg. I’m happy as my favorite ginger and love ginger so much. The chicken rice is nice and with an acceptable portion of chicken and nice to eat. I will come back and prolly order half chicken to bring home next time. You can try when passing by here.

reviews.author: L
reviews.text: I’ve eaten here COUNTLESS times. In fact, I was eating their steamed chicken rice every week for a while. Their chicken rice is consistently good and never disappoints. The price is pretty reasonable at $3.50 for a small set as well. There are many options for chicken rice at Redhill Food Centre so if you’re spoilt for choices, try this stall.

reviews.author: Soo Hin Yeoh
reviews.text:
From $3.50 a plate, this stall offers great value. Chicken is tender and fragrant from the sesame oil sprinkled.

Although queues are long, it’s never beyond a 5 to 10min wait. You can self-serve as much ginger or chilly sauce as much as you want

reviews.author: Ace Shadow
reviews.text:
Another dishonest stall to avoid at Redhill Close Food Centre.

Inconsistent serving of rice for dine-in customers as observed. I ordered above the standard portion and the rice portion was pathetic unlike the usual practice.

The guy was too stingy with the rice, unlike the previous lady.

Used to market them to acquaintance but not any more for such greedy and unscrupulous operators.

For the management:  Customers pay a higher price for the same food. Kindly be honest and not try to cut corners to reduce the quantity with the paid increased price! People eating there are obviously not tourists by the way. 😊

Another one on my banned list!

@Redhill Food Centre

reviews.author: Mark Yeo
reviews.text:
Had lunch on Sat at this stall (after Redhill hawker center was closed for 3 months from Oct to Dec 2025).
The steam chicken is really good.
====
The chicken rice is fragrant, yet it's not too oily and also not salty. We preferred roast chicken, and it tasted ok, and texture was tender, too.

reviews: Interesting stall. Their primary business is hotpot, but they use the same ingredients to whip up noodles... The star though was the wicked noodle seasoning.
stalls.stall_name: Faith & Aaron Mini Hotpot (Redhill Lane Block 85 Food Centre)

google.google_name: 迷你火锅 Faith & Aaron Mini Hotpot

reviews.author: Thomas Chan
reviews.text: Did not take picture, ordered 2 pax set, most impressed by the huge fresh prawns (ordered extra at the end). The soup tasted good, and all the ingredients was great. (Owner please keep it up) Came all the way from serangoon, was worth it. Thanks!

reviews.author: Jesslyn C
reviews.text: Inconsistent in service, bought takeaways from them several times. Sometimes they give prawns (which is in the menu) and other times suddenly the prawns are gone? And they charge the same price too, sometimes counting the amount wrongly as well.

reviews.author: Jas Lim
reviews.text: 1st try, ordered via grabfood. Amazing! It's Yummy, fresh, lots of ingredients, it's cheap for the freshness and portion given.  Totally worth it!  it deserves more than 5 stars!

reviews.author: HM L
reviews.text: Ordered from them via foodpanda a week ago, the portions are generous and ingredients are fresh. Will definitely order from them again.

reviews.author: Lyn Teng
reviews.text: Friendly boss and generous in portion of serving. Seafood mini hotpot served with fresh and big prawns.

reviews: Their coffee is pretty well liked... particularly fragrant. Worth a try, plus their opening hours are friendly.
While the salty butter is nice, the bread is too thick and is way too dry to eat.
stalls.stall_name: Uncle Mak Cafe (Redhill Lane Block 85 Food Centre)

reviews.author: Shaolin Leong
reviews.text:
Located at the first row facing Redhill Close .
Between Horfun & Char Siew stall. Friendly couple reasonable & decent kopi at $1.20 !

reviews.author: Reuben
reviews.text: Nicw stall with wide array of drinks. They also support the "pay it forward" scheme for the needy. Tea is pretty decent!

reviews.author: 葉
reviews.text: Old-school drinks stall with traditional breakfast sets, cold drinks, bottled teas, light bites, and sugar cane juice on the menu. There’s also a smaller list of hot drinks offered at a discount for seniors on the left of the stall counter.

reviews.author: CG
reviews.text: Pretty decent coffee. Not too acidic and easy to drink. Uncle and auntie running the stall was also nice. Set A at $3.4 is affordable.

reviews.author: CK C
reviews.text: This stall has a very cheerful and eye catching facade but, unfortunately, the traditional breakfast set was quite substandard. The teh siu dai was weak, the soft boiled eggs were overcooked and the bread was barely toasted.

reviews: No matter how full I am, must still buy Chicken wings from Yan Fried Bee Hoon. Tasty, crispy outside while inside juicy.
Bee Hoon ($0.60) can be mixed with different fried food... chicken wings was quite delicious and savoury.
stalls.stall_name: Yan Fried Bee Hoon (Redhill Lane Block 85 Food Centre)

reviews.author: Ling Sg
reviews.text:
This is an older market that I always enjoyed returning. Many delicious food choices. Any food one wants in a hawker centre, this one has it all.
Which one is nice? Look at the queues!

Don't forget to walk around the shops near it.

reviews.author: Hefferich Tan
reviews.text:
Wide variety of good food. Can be quite crowded during lunch peak period and difficult to get a table.

Had the best Satay Bee Hoon here. This SBH stall has a Michillin Bib Goumant. There is also an unusual Chee Chong Fun stall there that uses traditional stone milled rice flour to make the rice sheets. I ordered the Char Siew Chee Cheong fun. It was not the usual Chee Cheong Fun. It was very tasty and has lots of ingredients in it like, vegetables, minced pork meat, preserved radish besides the Char Siew. To top it off, they added an egg on top of the rice sheets. Well worth the $5.50. Very filling. It also comes with a different CCF sauce. This once has a thicker sauce with oyster sauce and soy sauce. It is accompanied with sambal chillie and a potent homemade chillie. Well worth a try when you are at Redhill Food Centre

reviews.author: YL Wong
reviews.text:
Started coming here more often vs Tiong Bahru Mkt becos the latter is getting more and more hip estate = more expensive.
This Redhill Mkt offers alot of good food under 1 roof.  And the best part is… the food portion vs the price is still considered value for money.
Some of the famous stalls are
- Roast Meat (facing road)
- Corner boiled soup (facing road)
- Fu Ming Carrot Cake
- Teochew Porridge stall in the middle
- Fried Chicken Wings/ Bee Hoon

reviews.author: Jinghorjiak
reviews.text: This is not my first time coming here. This food centre is a place to find many good local food. Standard of food here is quite high. Noodles, rices, xiaolongbao….etc. most all are above average. Will back again to try the food from other stalls.

reviews.author: Matthias Tan
reviews.text: Good variety of food stalls. After renovation in end 2025 some of the stalls have changed but still a good variety of quality stalls including standard chicken rice, roast meats, char kway teow, soup, Chinese noodles. Atmosphere is fine as always, not too hot with the fans and shade. Fairly near to Redhill mrt too.

reviews: Michelin Bib Gourmand... pretty popular for its Fried Carrot Cakes available in both white or black version.
The black carrot cake was sweet, savoury and spicy with a little bit of charred taste.
stalls.stall_name: Fu Ming Cooked Food (Redhill Lane Block 85 Food Centre)

reviews.author: Matthew Lee
reviews.text:
I arrived at this place at about noon, and when I looked at the stall without checking the operating hours, I thought they had already sold out for the day and was preparing to close shop, but when I enquired, the auntie told me they were just about to start and I thought I was in luck!
I checked the opening hours was supposed to start at 11am but they started about an hour later. I waited for them to prepare for operations while I walked around looking for other interesting food.
I just stepped away for a while and by the time I was back, uncle was already cooking the carrot cake and quite a number of orders were already taken. I also went to order and not sure if the auntie remembered me earlier, seemed like I didn't wait for long for the carrot cake to be served.
I had a mouthful of the carrot cake and all I could say was "oh my god so good". The wok hei was there,  the flavours on point and the carrot cake was so tender and smooth. The only bad point was I ordered a small portion and it was totally not satisfying for me.
Definitely worth the title of Michelin Bib Gourmand and this is probably the top 3 carrot cake I have eaten.
Worth a trip down and I will definitely be back to order a bigger portion and also both black and white versions.
Thumbs up!

reviews.author: Isaac Sam
reviews.text: This stall serves one of the better carrot cakes in town. The dish is well-fried with a robust smoky wok hei, generously spiced and full of flavour without being overly greasy. Each bite is savoury, punchy, and deeply satisfying. Simple, honest hawker fare done right—well worth the queue for carrot cake lovers.

reviews.author: L Chan
reviews.text:
Takeaway the black chai tow kway — fragrant, moist & soft on the inside, with a nicely crispy exterior. The sweet dark sauce is well balanced, not overly cloying & the wok hei really comes through.

Definitely better enjoyed fresh at the stall while it’s still hot & crisp. Simple, satisfying hawker comfort done right.

reviews.author: YL Wong
reviews.text:
Theu fren’s recommendations… I have been eating this stall for more than 10yrs.
I nvr like carrot cake that are hard.
If u are like me, appreciate REAL authentic soft carrot cake, this is the place.
Their carrot cake is moist and so soft, most of the times u need skillful chopsticks skills or a spoon to eat.  And every plate is patiently fried by a Snr couple. (Both of their skills are just as good)
Recently 2025 they won a Michelin Bib award, and gained more crowd coming fm other parts of Spore.   Else it was only those in the know or pple who lives around RedHill/ Henderson/ Tiong Bahru area.
Pls note… this stall opens only wef lunch or sometimes after lunch!  So don’t go too early!

reviews.author: Travel With Jack Chee
reviews.text:
There are quite a number of fried carrot cake stalls in the Redhill Food Centre. To those living in Redhill, you will know Fu Ming Cooked Food, which is affectionately known as the ‘Mickey Mouse’ stall as featured on its vintage signboard.

Despite the stall’s low-key, unpretentious appearance, you will be surprised that it was awarded the Michelin Bib Gourmand recognition in 2019, and has held this honour ever since.

The carrot cake here is sold in $3, $4, and $5 portions, in both black and white varieties, though black is the option you should definitely go for. Their radish cake is steamed and made fresh daily, which gives it a very soft and chewy texture, making it an easy meal for the elderly. Despite it being a little oily, I love how the fried carrot cake is moist inside, yet crispy on the outside. Fu Ming Cooked Food is definitely a gem here in the Redhill Food Centre.

reviews: Textbook. Every component from the rice to the tender ckn, is very simple and well executed.
The sio bak is the highlight here, it's got a thin hard crisp and it's very fatty so quite flavourful.
stalls.stall_name: Ru Ji Kitchen 如记小厨 (Rehill Lane)

google.google_name: Ru Ji Kitchen 如记小厨 (Redhill Lane)

reviews.author: Stanley Pang
reviews.text:
I prefer the style of fishball noodles here, which is the traditional chilli+vinegar+lard combo, rather than ketchup-y style.

Verdict: noodle cooked al dente but chilli had slight hint of bitterness. Lard is not crispy, maybe they didnt render it long enough. Soup is slightly bland but fishballs were handmade and taste great.

reviews.author: Delicious Myojo
reviews.text: A very satisfying meal in the morning. You can still savour the traditional taste of fish ball and meat balls here. The noodle usually sold out around 12pm depending on the crowd.

reviews.author: Kim Wee Soh
reviews.text:
There are quite a few Ru Ji Fishballs in Singapore, among them two of them related at Holland Dr and Ghim Mo apparently, Michelin recommended, and Toa Payoh and so on...

At Redhill market, there are also easily more than a handful of Fishball noodles stalls, and if you include the Bak Chor Mee stalls, you can easily choose your Mee Pok Ta from more than ten choices. But if you know your way around the best Mee Pok Ta from Ru Ji, there's only one choice here famed for their sambal chilli sauce, home made fishballs, fishcakes and definitely sold out by 1pm daily. From $3.50 to $5, this is easily affordable and great value. The Mee Pok is done al dente, the lardy sauce and crispy lard coutons fresh, the sambal is savoury and packs some real heat, and best of all, the fishballs are some of the best you can find here. Savoury yellowtail surimi, salty and airy, the original taste of handmade fishballs. Along with the thick cut fishcake slices fried in lard, add that with the lardy, sambal coated Mee Pok, this is easily better than some of the Michelin noodles or highly recommended Mee Pok Ta. Come get this, and hopefully it stays low profile as it is so keep the queue manageable

reviews.author: Henry Cheng
reviews.text: Tasty simple dishes are some of the hardest thing to get right. But this place does it really well. Ordered the fish ball noodles with extra ingredient (total $4) for my first time here. Fish cake and fish balls were very tasty, one of the best I have tasted in Singapore. The sambal cili that came with the noodles were a tad too spicy for me. Combined with the vinegar, you will have a very nice bowl of noodles to enjoy. Really worth a try if if you are at Redhill market.

reviews.author: Terence Lee
reviews.text:
Mee pok well cooked, texture just nice.
Fresh ingredients, handmade fishball texture are good.
Soup taste no bad.
Their sambal chilli sauce really give punches, great combination with the noodles.
People who can't take too spicy level can ask for less chilli sauce.
Reasonable pricing.
Worth eating.

reviews: The immediate difference here is the addition of bean sprouts, which lend a satisfying crunch... The white is more popular, with a smoky savoury sweet salty eggy flavour.
stalls.stall_name: Delicious Fried Carrot Cake (Redhill Lane Block 85 Food Centre)

google.google_name: Delicious Fried Carrot Cake 日夜香菜头粿 (#01-33)

reviews.author: derek neo
reviews.text: This is an old-school fried carrot cake. The black version is perfectly balanced - not too sweet, not too garlicky. I love the addition of bean sprouts which you can exclude if you want.

reviews.author: CP
reviews.text: If you like your carrot cake with a healthy twist, this one is not to be missed. Each plate is tailored - with or without beansprout, option for extra egg etc. It feels like eating crispy omelette and beansprout more than the radish cake. It is also one of the few stalls here that has grade A cleanliness. It feels like eating a complete meal rather than just the usual oily fried carrot cake.

reviews.author: Esmond Chun
reviews.text:
Wanted to eat fried carrot cake and found this stall review shown good (more than 4 🌟).
We ordered the black carrot cake $3.50, looking at the given quantity is acceptable. This stall given bean sprouts so when you eat can enjoying some "chewing". The carrot cake used is not too bad (not too hard or soft) and the owner putting sufficient black sweet sauce to make it nice  but just don't get the sufficient "woh hei".
Comparing with many other stalls I'm still very much like to give higher 🌟.

reviews.author: 葉
reviews.text: The white carrot cake here is recommended, with the layer of egg coating the layer carrot cake cubes fried to a crisp. Topped with optional bean sprouts, this dish has texture and serves as a dryer alternative to the wetter version at Fu Ming’s Cooked Food in the same hawker center.

reviews.author: Precious Han
reviews.text: This place serves up some seriously delicious fried carrot cake! The texture is just right, crispy on the outside and soft on the inside. Plus, the flavor is amazing. Definitely a must-try if you love carrot cake!

stalls.stall_name: Shi Le Yuan Teochew Porridge (Redhill Lane Block 85 Food Centre)

reviews.author: Sakura Stan
reviews.text:
Shi Le Yuan Kway Chap is a popular spot for traditional Teochew-style kway chap, known for its comforting flavours and well-braised ingredients. The stall serves a generous platter of braised items such as pork belly, pig intestines, tofu, tau pok, braised egg and preserved mustard greens, all simmered in a fragrant soy-based gravy. The sauce is rich and aromatic with a subtle herbal depth, coating each ingredient beautifully without being overly salty.

The pork belly is tender with a good balance of meat and fat, while the intestines are cleaned well and braised until soft and flavourful. Each item absorbs the savoury braising liquid, making every bite satisfying. The kway, which are broad rice sheets served in a light broth, are smooth and silky. The mild broth complements the richer braised platter, helping to balance the overall meal.

Portions are generous and prices remain reasonable, which explains the steady crowd during peak meal hours. Despite the simple presentation, the quality and consistency keep customers returning. For those craving a hearty and traditional kway chap in Singapore, Shi Le Yuan delivers a comforting bowl that highlights the beauty of this classic hawker dish.

reviews.author: Teck Chong Law
reviews.text:
One of my favourite kway chap stalls 👍👍

The queue speaks for itself and the regular customers know exactly how to order the food.

I have not been here for years and so glad that the food still tastes as good as before.

A bit expensive ($9.50) for the two pax portions plus three bowls of kway.  But I don't mind paying for it as I like the taste of the food and the kway.

They have adjusted their operations time, so I need to plan my next visit mindfully.

reviews.author: Marcus
reviews.text: Ordered 2 person portion. Portion size can see for yourself. Mid is an understatement. No da Chang no nothing, feels like they give based on mood. How is this Michelin LOL.

reviews.author: Merryheart
reviews.text: The Michelin Bib-Gourmand listed eatery had a humble, unassuming front, typical of many hawker centres in Singapore. The Kway Chap was served in a generous portion. The biggest win for me was the well cleaned intestines & pig tongue.  The stomach was melt-in-the mouth tender, served in a rich, flavourful dark soy-based broth. The rice noodles were silky smooth.

reviews.author: suzen
reviews.text:
First time trying at this kway chap stall. Since it has labelled of Michelin *. Too bad that the integrity of the owner is not having. For my case, the usual request no lean meat, no pig skin & no egg. This kind of portion that the boss still so daring to serve it out. Even his son was shocked in quiet. No justice at all.

Luckily thks to dbs paylah for the cash rebate. No more going back to this stall ever! 😤

stalls.stall_name: Hock Shun Traditional Curry (Redhill Lane Block 85 Food Centre)

google.google_name: Hock Shun Home-Made Claypot Curry Traditional 福顺砂锅咖喱

reviews.author: MNG
reviews.text:
A Chinese styled curry, moderately spiced. Flavor is mild and smooth in its curry texture. Suitable for those who cannot take much heat.

It came with a thigh and drumstick each as well as 2 potatoes. The items were prepared in advance and placed in clay pot to reheat. Unfortunately they were not as tender or soft as I expected and the curry did not penetrate through.

It did not hit the mark for me.

The market is quite rundown and dark. Hygiene can be improved.

reviews.author: Rach
reviews.text: Tried this after coming across the good reviews and boy, is the curry good!😋💯 added $1 for veg and I was surprised to see the claypot filled to the brim with a chunky drumstick/thigh, potato chunks, some taupok pieces and cabbage. Curry sauce tasted great and everything paired well nicely together. It was such a filling and tasty meal! Boss was nice and friendly too. Will be back when I’m in the area and craving curry chicken, for sure😊👍🏻

reviews.author: C K Leong (Ah Leong San)
reviews.text: Wanted to get takeaway from this stall but changed my mind when I realized it’s served in a claypot. Ordered bread to go with my yong tau foo curry and added $1 veggies. Bread came toasted and warm. Crusty top and soft inside. 👍🏼 when the piping hot pot of curry came it smelted great. It looks bland but it’s really tasty and reminds me of old school curry that was not overly spicy and salty. Because it was almost boiling hot the soft bread took in the curry immediately; soaking in the gravy right away. Yum! The bread top crust was definitely crusty to the bite soaked in curry. Though YTF was nothing to say about but with the curry it was delicious. Guess I’ll go back for the chicken parts some day.

reviews.author: Soo Hin Yeoh
reviews.text:
Hands down, this is the best Curry Chicken in Singapore, far better than Nana Curry. The stall always have a long queue and looks like local celebrities also patronise it from the pictures in the stall.

The portion is generous for my Chicken Drumstick set at $4.50, the gravy is creamy and addictive as you just want to drink it. Highly recommended 👍🏼👍🏼👍🏼

reviews.author: Timothy Low
reviews.text: Pretty decent  chicken curry served in a claypot. Choose either rice or bread to complete your meal, and also addition $1 tonadd vegetables.

stalls.stall_name: Traditional Hainanese Curry Rice (Redhill Lane Block 85 Food Centre)

google.google_name: Hong Seng Curry Rice 鸿森咖喱饭 @Redhill

reviews.author: Garrick Chow Rui Wen
reviews.text:
Been patronising this stall for a good 17-18 years and the taste has stuck to me. No longer staying in this neighbourhood but will still come by often just to have their curry rice😊. They just reopened after the hawker underwent some renovations and repair.

Ordered Pork chop, hei bi hiam, prawn fritters, cabbage, sunny side up egg with lots of curry sauce. Everytg was good😊Love it!

reviews.author: Tk Sim
reviews.text:
The service on a Tuesday evening was good even though there were many customers. I liked the two self-order kiosks where you can order your choice of food easily with clear pricing

You can pay using CDC vouchers, cash, PayNow etc

Look forward to returning again if I pass by the Redhill area

reviews.author: Nancy Lan Watkinson
reviews.text: I ordered the Curry Chicken Set which came with rice, a fried egg, cabbage and hae bee hiam (spicy fried shrimp). The dish was served warm, I think I’d prefer it to be hotter. The overall flavour was good, but not as spectacular as I imagined after hearing great things about this place.

reviews.author: Sean Goh
reviews.text:
Wanted to try something else, but the stall wasn't open that day. Walked around and noticed this stall that sells curry rice. Over here, they offer various sets with price starting from SGD$3.70 onwards. Apart from that you can order the dishes ala carte.

I ordered the value set, which was SGD$5.30. This set comes with a sunny side up egg, pork cutlet, braised vegetable, and fried prawn. The curry sauce was a huge disappointment. It was too way too salty and overpowering everything. Pork chop was okay, well marinated.

Overall, it's a pretty bad curry rice if you ask me. Would I return? Definitely not.

reviews.author: Shawn Dabi
reviews.text: Quite shiok. Ordered the 6.30 set. Uncle gave me a drumstick. Curry was thick, savoury and i mixed it with braised sauce and added the tofu. Good stuff!

stalls.stall_name: Tidjai Thai Food (Redhill Lane Block 85 Food Centre)

reviews.author: Mark Yeo
reviews.text:
New stall located at Redhill Cooked Food Market. And it's the only Thai food vendor.
We tried their deep fried seabass in thai chilli, green curry, spicy clams, garlic pork and kai lan. They use small chilli on most of their dishes which gives the "spicy kick". We like the food, especially our youngest kid who took studied Thai language and culture as one of her university modules and have been active in Thai mission work. She really enjoyed the meal ("a-roy" = delicious in Thai). We are simply just happy to be able to find nice Thai food at redhill market.

Tried their olive rice last night as a one dish meal for dinner. It was good. They added small red chilli aa garnish and became very spicy as the chilli seeds got mixed with the rice.

reviews.author: Feliz
reviews.text:
Horrible Horrible Thai food in Redhill market, not recommend to go this place for Thai food..

Bad experience with their food, I ordered green curry chicken, the curry watery with no taste n chicken rubbery, cost me $7 including fried egg .

Pleased proceed to Maxwell hawker center stall 85 (SISAKET Thai Food), if you really want Thai experience food cook by pure Thai person and now they became Spore PR.

This Tidjai Thai Food is the worst Thai food , they can't make the green curry chicken right then don't talk about the rest of the food, I don't even dare to order the rest.

Definitely Not recommended to this stall..

reviews.author: R G
reviews.text: The presentation of the olive rice was impressive (see pic). Taste is not bad as it is my first time trying this dish, am surprise to find cashew nuts hidden in the rice. Another i try is the green curry and told them to pump up the spice level and they truly makes my tummy and tongue rolling with happiness.

reviews.author: Jade Tay
reviews.text: Dine With My Mum. We Both Agreed That Food Was Quite Good. Esp The Chicken Wings. Bill Total $24.50. Lady Staff Was V Friendly Too.

reviews.author: R A
reviews.text: For the price is nice. Portion is decent. My friend took the pad Thai and she didn't like it. I liked my garlic pork though. Nice choice If you want something simple

stalls.stall_name: Allauddin's Briyani (Redhill Lane Block 85 Food Centre)

reviews.author: CHIN ANG
reviews.text: Tasty briyani cooked al dente aromatic as the ones at Beach road. Granted its cooked elsewhere but this does help one man show and food safety and consistency. Portions are great, enough veggies in soup, sour-sweet achar (not artificial vinegar), delicious flavored chicken curry slightly dry and long grains with enough spice and onions. Everything including peeled egg fresh, papadam finger licking. This new kid on the block will satisfy the briyani crave. What can be improved: rice lumps, mutton smell of soup & chicken curry made from powder. For takeaway, added fresh parsley greens @home even more appetizing.

reviews.author: MightyFoodie
reviews.text:
Was dining at Redhill Food Centre, saw a new signboard with a familiar name. Didn't know that one of my fav Nasi Briyani, Allauddin’s Briyani has a new outlet here

For those who are looking for a nice plate of Briyani around this area, this is worth a try.

Location: Allauddin’s Briyani, Redhill Food Centre, 85 Redhill Ln, Singapore 150085

reviews.author: Henry Cheng
reviews.text: Decided to takeaway chicken briyani from Redhill Market as I already had lunch so wanted to leave it for dinner instead. Was a very good choice. The chicken was fork tender and was cooked so perfectly in the curry like sauce. The briyani rice was really fragrant and wasn’t too heavy on the spices. Every mouthful of the briyani and the chicken was so wonderful. Definitely one of the top briyani on my list.

reviews.author: Zechs Merquise
reviews.text:
Was going to Tekka to eat but saw there was a store at red hill, I asked the guy runs the stall he said it’s the new store, this is on the Michelin guide, so I ordered the chicken set, honestly it’s quite good, maybe because I don’t eat it often, so it tasted quite good to me, I will recommend.
It’s a little spicy, beware!

reviews.author: 葉
reviews.text: Good, authentic briyani, served with curried chicken, fish or mutton, poppadom, a boiled egg, and a small bowl of curry gravy. Portion is generous and certainly filling enough for a meal.

stalls.stall_name: Ali Fatimah Food Stall (Redhill Lane Block 85 Food Centre)

reviews.author: S L
reviews.text: Was lucky on a Tues morning and only had to queue less than 10mins for my breakfast. The freshly-made prata is crispy and fluffy indeed. The default mutton curry gravy is a little watery for my taste though.

reviews.author: Feliz
reviews.text:
This prata shop has been passed on from father to son. Mr Sultan is one of the son that operate his business on Sunday, Monday and Tuesday starting from 10.30pm to the next morning till all the prata sold out. It can be as early as before 9am all sold out.

Yes , indeed the prata is fluffy and not oily.
Whenever I visit the shop , I will buy 1 egg onions prata and 2 kosong (plain prata). I ate 3 pieces by myself, is so good.

Egg onion prata is $2 per piece and Plain prata is $1 per piece , total I pay $4.

Do come and try ,if you can come from Sunday to Tuesday is way better because the other son take over from Wednesday to Sunday morning, his curry so so only.

But Sunday 10.30pm make by Mr Sultan the curry is way way much better . This is my honest opinion.

So do make your trip to Blk 85 Redhill lane, Stall #01-01.

Enjoy.👍😋

reviews.author: Peter Lin
reviews.text: Kosong Prata and Egg Prata. Why are all the popular pratas opened only in the morning? Yes we have the upper thomson prata which is great for night spots and supper but the really good fluffy ones are often only open during the day such as this one. My favourite dip is the sugar!

reviews.author: MightyFoodie
reviews.text:
This is one of the good prata places that is worth trying. Their pratas are crispy, fluffy, and not too oily, pair with curry and sugar. Yumz.

Location: Ali Fatimah Food Stall, 85 Redhill Ln, #01-01, Singapore 150085

reviews.author: Andrew Stevens
reviews.text:
Excellent roti prata. Had to queue for about 30minutes at around 9am but definitely worth the wait.

Nice to see these being freshly flattened out and grilled too, a nice bit of authentic entertainment.

Light and fluffy roti prata with an deep and rich curry sauce. We ordered 3 pieces between two people which was plenty. Two of them were plain, which were the best. The egg one was a bit too thick and stodgy, I'd not bother with that again.

With the curry you can get a piece of mutton, which next time I'll definitely do as it looked tender and amazing.

Definitely going back. Get there early to avoid disappointment!

stalls.stall_name: Redhill Curry Rice (Redhill Lane Block 85 Food Centre)

reviews.author: Ling Sg
reviews.text:
This is an older market that I always enjoyed returning. Many delicious food choices. Any food one wants in a hawker centre, this one has it all.
Which one is nice? Look at the queues!

Don't forget to walk around the shops near it.

reviews.author: Hefferich Tan
reviews.text:
Wide variety of good food. Can be quite crowded during lunch peak period and difficult to get a table.

Had the best Satay Bee Hoon here. This SBH stall has a Michillin Bib Goumant. There is also an unusual Chee Chong Fun stall there that uses traditional stone milled rice flour to make the rice sheets. I ordered the Char Siew Chee Cheong fun. It was not the usual Chee Cheong Fun. It was very tasty and has lots of ingredients in it like, vegetables, minced pork meat, preserved radish besides the Char Siew. To top it off, they added an egg on top of the rice sheets. Well worth the $5.50. Very filling. It also comes with a different CCF sauce. This once has a thicker sauce with oyster sauce and soy sauce. It is accompanied with sambal chillie and a potent homemade chillie. Well worth a try when you are at Redhill Food Centre

reviews.author: YL Wong
reviews.text:
Started coming here more often vs Tiong Bahru Mkt becos the latter is getting more and more hip estate = more expensive.
This Redhill Mkt offers alot of good food under 1 roof.  And the best part is… the food portion vs the price is still considered value for money.
Some of the famous stalls are
- Roast Meat (facing road)
- Corner boiled soup (facing road)
- Fu Ming Carrot Cake
- Teochew Porridge stall in the middle
- Fried Chicken Wings/ Bee Hoon

reviews.author: Jinghorjiak
reviews.text: This is not my first time coming here. This food centre is a place to find many good local food. Standard of food here is quite high. Noodles, rices, xiaolongbao….etc. most all are above average. Will back again to try the food from other stalls.

reviews.author: Matthias Tan
reviews.text: Good variety of food stalls. After renovation in end 2025 some of the stalls have changed but still a good variety of quality stalls including standard chicken rice, roast meats, char kway teow, soup, Chinese noodles. Atmosphere is fine as always, not too hot with the fans and shade. Fairly near to Redhill mrt too.

stalls.stall_name: Green Sky Dessert (Redhill Lane Block 85 Food Centre)

google.google_name: Green Sky Dessert 青天冷热甜品

reviews.author: T greensake
reviews.text:
5/August/2025
Had brown sugar ice kacang ($2.30) for dessert, was good. Have been patronizing this stall since I was a kid. They serve hot desserts too. Fast friendly service.

reviews.author: WT Wong
reviews.text: A bowl of chendol that truly hits the spot – rich, creamy coconut milk balanced with fragrant gula melaka, tender green jelly strands, and just the right amount of shaved ice. Refreshing and cooling on a sweltering day, and at a price that makes it excellent value for money.

reviews.author: J. P
reviews.text: Had this chendol on a hot afternoon and it was great! The lady boss was generous with the ingredients. Really great value for $2. Do go and support!

reviews.author: Soo Hin Yeoh
reviews.text:
Not to be confused with the Green Sky Desert Stall at Redhill Market, this desert stall at Bukit Merah View with the same name is a great option.

Their pricing remains o e of the lowest in Singapore with Cheng Th’ng at $1.50 and Ice Kachang at $1.70. Stall accepts QR Code pay and CDC vouchers.

Recommended 👍🏼

reviews.author: Super Fragrance
reviews.text:
Chendol cost $1.70. cheaper than market rate.

I must said it is one of the most unsightly chendol I have seen. But luckily the taste decent. Just right sweetness. Coconut not overwhelming. But seems to be missing gela melaka.
